Flammability of some ornamental species in wildland–urban interfaces in southeastern France: laboratory assessment at particle level. Environmental Management, 2013, 52, p. 467 - p. 480.  10.1007/s00267-013-0067-z .  hal-00941751 

A. Ganteaume, M. Jappiot, C. Lampin. 2012. Assessing the flammability of surface fuels beneath ornamental vegetation in wildland-urban interfaces in Provence (South-eastern France). International Journal of Wildland Fire, CISRO Publishing. 22(3), 333-42.

data.name: Fine Gardening
data.attribution: Adriene Edwards and Rachel Schleiger. 2023. Firescaping: Plants That Help Protect Your Property From Fire; A Manual for Readiness in Wildfire Country. Timber Press, Portland, OR.
data.notes: Great general fire safe gardening and native plant care reference. Integrated a majority of the plants listed. Because placement and spacing best practices are not recommended on a plant by plant basis, and several highly flammable plants are listed it was impossible to spatially integrate based on hazard ignition zones.

data.name: Fire Performance Plant Selector

data.name: Fire Safe Marin
data.topicsAddressed: Flammable plants, spacing, landscape design, drought tolerant plants, mulches

data.notes: Great website available for all topics pertaining to developing fire adapted buildings and landscapes!

data.name: Friends of Corte Madera Creek
data.topicsAddressed: Plants that are very problematic & their California native alternatives which are not low flammability.
data.attribution:
What Not to Plant by Laura Lovett. 2018. Friends of Corte Madera Creek Watershed Friends of Corte Madera Creek Watershed P.O. Box 415, Lark-
spur, California 94977
data.notes: Cited a few statements about some problem plants. Leymus condensatus is mislabled as Elymus condensatus.

data.name: GardenSoft
data.topicsAddressed: Plants on Ashland Oregon's Waterwise Plants List
data.notes: GardenSoft is under contract with the City of Ashland, Oregon and other municipalities in other states to provide interactive Internet access to plant lists. In Ashland it serves as the City's official "Waterwise" plant list. Many of the plants are misidentified as  "waterwise" and "fire resistant". A fair number are noxious weeds. Use this resource with caution.
